summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #713: pass sender name (from Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, etc.) through to the LLM by adding an optional `name` field to `ChatMessage::User` and threading it through all conversion and routing paths.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented the feature across 22 files: added `name` field to `ChatMessage::User`, updated all pattern matches and constructors, threaded sender_name from channel params through `run_streaming`/`run_with_tools`/`run_agent_loop_streaming`, prepended `[name]: ` for Anthropic (which lacks native name support), and extracted sender names from persisted session JSON. All 562 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.

repo: ChatMessage is defined in crates/agents/src/model.rs and used across agents, providers, chat, and swift-bridge crates — any enum variant change requires touching ~14+ files
Anthropic provider doesn't support a native `name` field on user messages; the workaround is to prepend `[name]: ` to content text
OpenAI Chat Completions API natively supports `name` on user messages via JSON field injection in `to_openai_value()`
Persisted session JSON stores channel metadata under a `channel` key with `sender_name` and/or `username` subfields; `values_to_chat_messages()` in model.rs is the extraction point for history replay
The send path forks at `stream_only`: `run_streaming()` for streaming-only, `run_with_tools()` for tool-enabled; both need sender_name threaded through
Agent loop entries (runner/streaming.rs, runner/non_streaming.rs) represent framework-generated messages, not channel messages — `name: None` is correct there
Legacy test copies in `crates/agents/src/runner/tests_legacy/` are self-contained and don't use the main module's runner signatures
`send_sync` in chat_impl.rs sets `channel: None`, so sender_name is always None on that path
CUDA build failures on macOS are a known environment issue unrelated to code changes; test individual modified crates instead of full workspace

code.finding: ChatMessage::User variant — added `name: Option<String>` field here
values_to_chat_messages() extracts sender_name from val.get("channel")["sender_name"] or ["username"] for history replay
to_openai_value() injects `name` into JSON when Some — OpenAI supports this natively
Anthropic user message handler — prepends `[name]: ` to content text as workaround for missing native name support
Current message ChatMessage::User construction — now takes sender_name param and sets name field
Sender name extracted from params["channel"]["sender_name"] or ["username"] here, then passed to run_streaming/run_with_tools
run_with_tools() threads sender_name down to run_agent_loop_streaming() for current message naming
run_agent_loop_streaming() now accepts sender_name: Option<String> and applies it to the initial ChatMessage::User construction
Third ChatMessage::User construction missed by replace_all due to different indentation depth — required manual fix

workflow: When adding a field to a Rust enum variant, run `cargo check` early to get a complete list of all pattern match failures before fixing them one by one
Use `replace_all` cautiously for struct literal fixes — indentation differences can cause silent misses; verify with grep after
Thread optional parameters (like sender_name) through function signatures rather than using globals or thread-locals to maintain explicitness
Run tests on specific modified crates (`cargo test -p agents -p chat -p providers`) when full workspace build is blocked by unrelated environment issues (CUDA on macOS)

friction: Multiple rounds of reading model.rs were needed (6+ reads) before starting edits — the file is large and required navigating to specific line ranges repeatedly
replace_all on ChatMessage::User constructions in swift-bridge/src/chat.rs silently missed one occurrence due to different indentation, only caught by cargo check
The function signature threading chain was longer than expected: send.rs → run_streaming/run_with_tools → run_agent_loop_streaming — each step required separate edits and verification
Full workspace `just test` blocked on macOS by llama.cpp/CUDA build failure unrelated to the changes; had to test individual crates instead
Anthropic test constructions required 6 separate Edit calls to add `name: None` because replace_all couldn't be used safely across all unique contexts
open_items: Web chat sender identity not implemented — web chat doesn't use ChannelMessageMeta; supporting username display for web sessions requires auth context (explicitly out of scope)
genai crate name support not implemented — the genai abstraction may not support `name`; currently ignores it with `name: _` (explicitly out of scope)

Non-streaming agent loop path (run_agent_loop_with_context in non_streaming.rs) does not receive sender_name — only the streaming path was wired up; if non-streaming channel messages exist they won't get names
Manual verification pending: send a Telegram group message and confirm LLM response references sender name; check persisted session JSON for channel.sender_name presence
